replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - for sure mate - I am tempted lol

author: @zylascope
text: Great. Can't wait to see you drive it around London :)

replies.author: @charlesdickens6706
replies.text: .....it's unlikely to be permitted these days .Clive Sinclair did in late seventies manufacture a batch of low height pedal electric vehicles with a short range , his folly was discussed in various articles of the day , New Scientist for one example . Something like the twizzy of today makes more sense despite no pedals . without pedals .

replies.author: @zylascope
replies.text: @charlesdickens6706ah yes, I was thinking about the Sinclair car. I was told that you didn't need to register them for use on the road. I met a fellow in Australia, years ago, who had one. I helped him carry it out of his ute and into his shed. He told me he thought it would be worth a lot of money one day.

replies.author: @charlesdickens6706
replies.text: @zylascope ....they could be worth a little something in future. I have a figure in my head that only seven hundred were produced . He was obsessed with making things as small as possible like the stereo systems and the zx81 ,many people didn't buy his stuff precisely cos it was too small . His main legacy is the handheld calculator cos folk found it to be a convenient miniaturisation of a desktop item of the day .

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have been pondering getting it road legal - it doesn't take much for a one off and the class is light quad - so you wouldn't even need a licence !

author: @chuxxsss
text: Thanks Rob, hit a tree stump a month ago, and a guide fell off could not see where it was at the time.  Now I can see where to look as the belt comes off some times. Have a great day both of you from Tuesday morning Australia thanks again.

author: @totherarf
text:
And another piece of the jigsaw done!

author: @ShilohSedlak
text: Why would you want to have it ran by belts when it is electric motor driven? That seems like a horrible waste of energy.. You should get a pwm motor control and use that to regulate your speed and you should mount the motor directly to the front of the rearend. If that is geared to high then you should buy a gear reduction box off ebay for $70 and put that coupled to the motor coupled to the rearend.. You can even get them L shaped reduction boxes if you desire.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: like anything mate there are always a hundred ways to go - I like what you suggest for sure but I want to retain as much as possible

author: @dav1dbone
text: Great project Rob, just a thought, once you get it up and running and decide to sell it, because let's face it space is a premium in the workshop, you might consider adding/reinstating the grass cutting feature - have you seen how much they go for? Not to mention how handy it would be, also petrol engines can be a pain to start and maintain, smelly, noisy and dangerous.

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: If you added the wind generator and an auxiliary battery you could extend the range and convince onlookers that it's a combine harvester! A solar cab roof?

author: @charlesdickens6706
text: .....are you looking at retaining the transmission? The transmission does have a differential but a differential action is also effected by simply two individual electric drive motors connected in parallel .

author: @ollieb9875
text:
Like a bought one 👍😀 
I got a free welder from a neighbour moving house. I was looking for stick welder like yours but this is gasless MIG 😲 using flux core wire. I am dubious 😅 still, free is free, with clamps, gloves, mask and an angle grinder 🙃

replies.author: @ollieb9875
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I think the grinder may come in handy 😋 it's very basic welder from the look of it though.. I'm searching for some bits of steel to give it a go 👍😀 cheers boss 😅
